title: 10 best herbal teas for throat congestion

remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Purple coneflower tea contains antioxidants that help reduce inflammation and soothe irritated throats, making it easier to manage symptoms of congestion.
Anti-inflammatory compounds (e.g., cinnamic acid): These compounds in the tea help reduce swelling and ease discomfort associated with throat congestion, allowing for easier breathing and swallowing.
Echinacein: A bioactive compound found in purple coneflower, echinacein has anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that can help combat viral infections that cause throat congestion.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea, also known as purple coneflower, teas helps with throat congestion because of its anti-inflammatory properties and ability to boost the immune system. The tea's active compounds, such as alkylamides and caffeic acid, help to reduce swelling and fight off viral and bacterial infections that cause congestion. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea's antiviral properties help to soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes, providing quick relief from sore throats and congestion. This natural remedy is often used to alleviate cold and flu symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Cichoric Acid: A type of phenolic acid, cichoric acid helps reduce inflammation and soothe the throat, making it easier to breathe and swallow when congested.
Caffeic Acid: A polyphenolic compound, caffeic acid has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate throat congestion by reducing swelling and promoting healthy tissue recovery.

Alkylamides: A class of non-nitrogenous compounds, alkylamides in Echinacea purpurea have immunomodulatory effects that help regulate the body's immune response and reduce symptoms associated with throat congestion, such as coughing and soreness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are commonly used because of their high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are known to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate throat congestion.

Flowers: They are often used to make teas for throat congestion because of their high concentration of alkylamides, which have been shown to have immunomodulatory effects that can help boost the body's defense against infections.

Roots: They are frequently used due to their rich content of glycosides and other compounds that have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help soothe and protect the throat from congestion and infection.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 ounces of fresh echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves in late summer or early fall.
Dry the harvested plant material in a low-temperature oven at 105 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind 1-2 teaspoons of dried echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Steep 1 teaspoon of the echinacea powder in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to make a tea.
Strain the tea and serve immediately or store it in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Thyme's antibacterial compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, help combat bacterial infections that can cause throat congestion, reducing inflammation and relieving symptoms.
Expectorant properties: Thyme's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up and alleviate congestion in the throat.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help reduce inflammation and swelling in the throat, providing relief from soreness and discomfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris, also known as thyme, teas helps with throat congestion because of its expectorant properties, which facilitate the removal of mucus from the respiratory tract. The active compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, possess antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce the swelling and kill the bacteria causing the infection. As a result, thyme tea helps to soothe and clear the throat, providing relief from coughs and congestion, making it a popular remedy for respiratory issues.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Thymol acts as an expectorant, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the throat, making it easier to breathe and relieving congestion.
Caryophyllene oxide: Caryophyllene oxide exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling and inflammation in the throat, providing relief from discomfort and congestion.
Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid has antimicrobial properties, which can help combat infections that may be contributing to throat congestion, such as a sore throat or tonsillitis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of thymol, a compound with antimicrobial and expectorant properties.
Flowers: The flowers are used to add a flavorful and aromatic touch to teas, while also providing some relief from throat congestion with their mild antimicrobial properties.
Buds: The buds are used in some herbal teas for their ability to reduce inflammation and soothe the throat, although less commonly used than leaves and flowers.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thyme leaves from the garden or store-bought container by pinching off the stems.
Dry the harvested thyme leaves in a single layer on a paper towel at room temperature for 1-2 hours.
Combine 1 teaspoon of dried thyme leaves with 1 cup of boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the thyme tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove loose thyme particles.
Discard the thyme leaves and serve the tea hot or chilled according to personal preference immediately.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginkgo biloba contains flavonoids and terpenoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, providing relief from congestion.
Expectorant properties: The tea's saponins and sesquiterpenes stimulate the production of mucus, making it easier to expel excess phlegm and mucous from the throat, helping to alleviate congestion.

Antimicrobial activity: Ginkgo biloba's extracts have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial properties against certain bacteria and viruses that can cause respiratory infections, which may contribute to throat congestion.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba, also known as maidenhair tree, teas helps with throat congestion because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The flavonoids and terpenoids in Ginkgo biloba work to reduce swelling in the throat and nasal passages, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, the tea's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out mucus, allowing for improved airflow and relief from congestion. This makes Ginkgo biloba tea a popular natural remedy for soothing a sore throat and alleviating respiratory issues.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids (especially Quercetin and Kaempferol): These flavonoids have potent anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and inflammation in the throat, making it easier to breathe and alleviate congestion.
Bilobalide: This triterpene has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help soothe and calm the throat, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by congestion.

Ginkgolides (especially Ginkgolide B): These sesquiterpenes have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which can help combat respiratory infections and reduce congestion by breaking down excess mucus and opening airways.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Ginkgo biloba are used to make teas for throat congestion due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and reduce swelling in the throat.
Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds are used to make teas for throat congestion due to their expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the throat.
Buds: Ginkgo biloba buds are used to make teas for throat congestion due to their antibacterial properties, which help to combat infections and reduce inflammation in the throat.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 ginkgo biloba leaves from a mature tree in late summer or early fall.
Dry the leaves in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Combine 1 teaspoon of dried ginkgo biloba leaves with 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the flavors and properties to infuse.
Strain the tea and serve immediately, discarding the solids and enjoying the warm beverage.

remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum tea helps loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel and relieve congestion.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling in the mucous membranes of the throat, relieving discomfort and pain associated with congestion.
Antimicrobial properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum tea contains antimicrobial compounds that help combat bacterial and viral infections causing throat congestion, promoting a faster recovery.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus, also known as Tasmanian blue gum, teas helps with throat congestion because of its decongestant properties. The tea contains eucalyptol, a natural compound that helps to break down and clear mucus from the airways. This allows for easier breathing and alleviates discomfort caused by congestion. By reducing inflammation and promoting drainage, the tea helps to relieve coughs and sore throats, providing quick relief for those suffering from respiratory issues. Regular consumption can also help to soothe and calm the throat.
remedy_constituents: Eucalyptol: This terpene acts as a natural expectorant, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the throat, making it easier to breathe.
Cineole (also known as Eucalyptol): This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, providing relief from congestion.
Rifaterpinene: This terpene has decongestant properties, helping to open airways and reduce mucus production, making it easier to breathe and relieving throat congestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of eucalyptol, a natural decongestant that helps relieve throat congestion.
Buds: The buds are used for their high eucalyptol content, which helps to open airways and reduce inflammation in the throat.
Barks: The barks are used for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to soothe and protect the throat from infections and irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves in the early morning for optimal oil content and flavor.
Dry the harvested leaves in a low-temperature oven at 95°f for 2 hours to preserve their potency.
Grind 1 teaspoon of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder for easy infusion.
Steep 1 teaspoon of ground eucalyptus globulus powder in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve and discard the solids to enjoy the herbal infusion.

remedy_properties: Menthol: Menthol helps to thin mucus, making it easier to expel from the body and reducing congestion in the throat.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, teas helps with throat congestion because of its natural decongestant properties. The menthol in peppermint helps to thin out mucus, making it easier to expel, which in turn reduces pressure on the throat. Additionally, peppermint's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm the throat, reducing irritation and discomfort. As a result, drinking peppermint tea can provide quick relief from throat congestion, making it a popular remedy for colds and respiratory issues.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Acts as a natural expectorant, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the throat, thereby reducing congestion and soreness.
Eugenol: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the throat, making it easier to breathe and swallow.
Caryophyllene: Possesses analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate throat pain and discomfort associated with congestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are commonly used due to their high menthol content, which helps to relax the muscles in the throat and relieve congestion.
Stems: Stems of Mentha x piperita are used as they contain a similar concentration of menthol and other compounds that help to ease congestion and soothe the throat.

Flowers: Although less commonly used than leaves and stems, the flowers of Mentha x piperita are still utilized for their mild antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in reducing throat inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of fresh leaves from mentha x piperita plants in the early morning.
Rinse the leaves gently with cold water to remove any dirt or debris.
Steep 1 teaspoon of fresh or dried leaves in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve to remove the leaves.
Add honey to taste to balance the flavor of the tea.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Sage contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the throat, alleviating discomfort caused by congestion.
Mucolytic agents: Sage's saponins and terpenes have been shown to break down mucus and thin its consistency, making it easier to expel from the body and reducing congestion and coughing.

Antimicrobial properties: Sage's essential oils, particularly thujone and camphor, possess antimicrobial properties that can help combat bacterial and viral infections responsible for throat congestion, promoting a healthy throat environment.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis, also known as sage, teas helps with throat congestion because of its expectorant properties, which help loosen and clear mucus from the airways. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties also reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, providing relief from coughing and soreness. Additionally, the antioxidants present in sage tea help combat infections and promote overall respiratory health, allowing for easier breathing and a faster recovery from congestion. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to soothe and calm irritated throats.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic Acid: A polyphenolic compound that helps to reduce inflammation and congestion in the throat by its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Salvianolic Acids: A group of polyphenolic compounds with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the throat, thereby alleviating congestion.
Thymol: A terpene with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that helps to reduce the growth of bacteria and other pathogens in the throat, thereby reducing congestion and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Salvia officinalis are used to make teas for throat congestion because of their high content of volatile oils and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help soothe and calm the throat.

Buds: The buds of Salvia officinalis are used to make teas for throat congestion because they contain high amounts of flavonoids and saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that help loosen and clear mucus from the throat.

Stems: The stems of Salvia officinalis are used to make teas for throat congestion because they contain high amounts of tannins and flavonoids, which have astringent and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and soothe the throat.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried salvia officinalis leaves and 1 cup of boiling water for the tea base.
Steep the dried leaves in the boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes to release the flavors.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine mesh sieve into a cup or teapot to remove solids.
Add honey or sugar to taste if desired to sweeten the herbal tea.
Serve the tea immediately and enjoy within 30 minutes for optimal flavor and benefits.

remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and soothe irritation in the throat, providing relief from congestion and coughs.
Licoricidin: This flavonoid has antimicrobial properties, which help to combat the underlying infections that cause throat congestion, such as the common cold or sinusitis.

Glycosides: The glycosides present in licorice tea have anti-inflammatory and demulcent properties, which create a protective barrier on the mucous membranes of the throat, helping to soothe and calm irritated tissues and reduce inflammation.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, teas helps with throat congestion because it contains anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin and flavonoids. These compounds help to reduce inflammation and swelling in the throat, making it easier to breathe and swallow. The soothing properties of licorice tea also help to calm irritation and coughs, providing relief from throat congestion. Additionally, the expectorant properties of licorice tea help to loosen and clear mucus, further relieving congestion and promoting a more comfortable and healthy throat.
remedy_constituents: Licorice Saponins (Glycyrrhizin): These saponins have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, providing relief from congestion.
Flavonoids (Quercetin, Kaempferol): These flavonoids have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which help to reduce inflammation and kill pathogens that can cause throat congestion.
Terpenoids (Liquiritin, Isoliquiritin): These terpenoids have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helping to break up and clear mucus from the throat, thereby relieving congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties and can help soothe throat congestion.
Leaves: Contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, making them useful for relieving throat irritation.
Barks: Have been traditionally used to make teas that can help reduce inflammation and calm the throat, due to their content of glycosides and phenolic compounds.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1-2 teaspoons of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots for every 8 oz of water required.
Heat 8 oz of water to a temperature of 212 degrees fahrenheit in a saucepan.
Steep the dried roots in the heated water for 5-10 minutes to release the flavors.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove the solids.
Allow the tea to cool to a comfortable temperature for consumption within 5 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as taraxasterol and sesquiterpenes, help reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort in the throat, making it easier to breathe and swallow.
Expectorant properties: The tea's saponins and flavonoids help loosen and expel mucus from the airways, relieving congestion and coughs, while also soothing and calming irritated mucous membranes.

Antioxidant properties: Dandelion tea's antioxidants, such as vitamin C and beta-carotene, help protect the throat tissues from oxidative damage caused by free radicals, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale, also known as dandelion, teas helps with throat congestion because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The tea's active compounds, such as sesquiterpene lactones and taraxasterol, work to reduce swelling and soothe the mucous membranes in the throat, making it easier to breathe and swallow. Additionally, the tea's diuretic properties help to clear mucus and reduce fluid buildup, providing quick relief from congestion and coughs. Its expectorant properties also aid in thinning mucus, making it easier to expel.
remedy_constituents: Apolyphenones: Apolyphenones, a type of phenolic compound, in Taraxacum officinale teas have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and soothe irritated tissues in the throat, thereby alleviating congestion.

Taraxasterols: Taraxasterols, a group of triterpene saponins, exhibit expectorant properties, facilitating the removal of mucus and other secretions from the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe and relieving throat congestion.

Fatty acids: Fatty acids present in Taraxacum officinale teas, such as oleic and linoleic acids, have anti-inflammatory and mucolytic properties, which help break down mucus and reduce inflammation in the throat, thereby alleviating congestion and promoting easier breathing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high concentration of taraxasterol, a compound that helps reduce inflammation and alleviate throat congestion.
Flowers: They are used because of their high content of taraxasterol and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties to soothe a sore throat.
Roots: They are used due to their rich content of inulin, a prebiotic that can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of throat congestion.
remedy_recipe: Gather taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers for a total of 2 teaspoons of dried plant material.
Heat 8 ounces of water in a pot to a temperature of 212 degrees fahrenheit for 5 minutes.
Steep the taraxacum officinale in the hot water for 5 to 7 minutes to release its active ingredients.
Strain the tea through a fine mesh sieve into a cup to remove the plant material.
Allow the tea to cool to a temperature of 98 degrees fahrenheit before consumption for 10 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Leafy spurge tea's antioxidant properties help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the throat, which can contribute to congestion relief.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in leafy spurge tea can help to reduce swelling and discomfort associated with throat congestion, making it easier to breathe and swallow.

Expectorant properties: Leafy spurge tea's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the throat, making it easier to expel and relieving congestion symptoms such as coughing and wheezing.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Euphorbia esula, also known as leafy spurge, teas helps with throat congestion because of its rich anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The herbal tea contains flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds that help to reduce swelling and ease the discomfort associated with a sore throat. By thinning mucus and promoting its expulsion, Euphorbia esula tea alleviates congestion and soothes the throat, providing fast relief from coughs, sore throats, and respiratory issues. remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Euphorbia esula teas help with throat congestion by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the throat, thereby alleviating swelling and discomfort.

Phenolic acids (e.g., ferulic acid): Phenolic acids in Euphorbia esula teas exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce throat congestion by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and free radicals.
Saponins: Saponins in Euphorbia esula teas may help to break down and clear mucus from the throat, making it easier to breathe and relieving congestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Euphorbia esula leaves are used to make teas due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help to calm a sore throat.
Roots: Euphorbia esula roots are used to make teas due to their expectorant properties that help to loosen and clear mucus from the throat.
Rhyzomes: Euphorbia esula rhyzomes are used to make teas due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help to reduce swelling and combat infections in the throat.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of fresh euphorbia esula leaves and stems at dawn when moisture levels are highest.
Dry the harvested euphorbia esula in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind 5-7 grams of dried euphorbia esula into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Steep 1 teaspoon of the ground euphorbia esula powder in 250 milliliters of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the euphorbia esula tea through a cheesecloth and serve immediately after preparation is complete.

remedy_properties: Mucilage content: Mullein tea contains mucilage, a thick, protective substance that helps soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes in the throat, reducing inflammation and discomfort.

Antiviral and antibacterial properties: The saponins present in mullein tea have been shown to exhibit antiviral and antibacterial activity, helping to combat infections that can cause throat congestion and promoting a healthy respiratory tract.
Expectorant properties: Mullein tea has expectorant properties, which means it helps loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion and discomfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Verbascum thapsus, also known as mullein, teas helps with throat congestion because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties and soothing effects on the mucous membranes. The tea's high content of saponins, flavonoids, and mucilages creates a protective barrier on the throat, reducing inflammation and irritation. This helps to loosen and clear excess mucus, providing quick relief from coughs, colds, and respiratory issues. Mucilage: This thick, protective substance forms a physical barrier on the mucous membranes, helping to soothe and calm irritated tissues, which in turn can reduce throat congestion.
Flavonoids: These plant compounds possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce swelling and combat underlying infections that contribute to throat congestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Verbascum thapsus are used to make teas for throat congestion because of their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the throat.

Roots: The roots of Verbascum thapsus are used to make teas for throat congestion because they are rich in saponins, which have expectorant properties that help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried verbascum thapsus flowers and leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Clean 1 tablespoon of dried verbascum thapsus or 2 tablespoons of fresh verbascum thapsus to remove any dirt.
Steep 1 tablespoon of dried verbascum thapsus in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release its properties.
Strain the verbascum thapsus tea through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove solids.
Serve the verbascum thapsus tea hot or iced immediately to enjoy its flavor and benefits.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal teas helps with throat congestion because its active compound, berberine, has natural antimicrobial properties that combat the underlying infections causing the congestion. The tea also contains other bioactive compounds like alkaloids and glycosides that help reduce inflammation and soothe the mucous membranes in the throat, making it easier to expel excess mucus and breathe easily. remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the goldenseal plant are rich in berberine, a natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compound that helps soothe and calm irritated throats.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes, or underground stems, of the goldenseal plant contain a high concentration of alkaloids that have antibacterial properties, making them effective in reducing congestion and inflammation in the throat.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried goldenseal root powder into a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Heat water to a temperature of 212°f and pour it over the goldenseal root powder.
Allow the mixture to steep for five minutes before straining the tea into another cup.
Add honey to taste if desired, and then drink the tea immediately while still warm.
Repeat the process three times a day as needed until symptoms of throat congestion improve.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality plantago major leaves and dry them thoroughly in a warm, well-ventilated area for several days.
Combine one teaspoon of dried plantago major leaves with boiling water in a heat-resistant cup to create a soothing tea.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes to release the bioactive compounds and essential oils from the plant material.
Strain the tea into a separate cup using a fine-mesh strainer or cheesecloth to remove any solids and sediment.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh red clover flowers and leaves, ensuring they are clean and free of debris.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried red clover flowers for every cup of boiling water you plan to use.
Heat water in a pot to a boil and then reduce the heat to a simmer for five minutes.
Add the measured red clover flowers to the hot water and allow it to steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary teas helps with throat congestion because of its natural decongestant properties. The herbs in rosemary tea, such as camphor and borneol, work to ease congestion by thinning mucus secretions, making it easier to expel and reducing the thickening of phlegm. Expectorant properties: Rosemary's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the throat, making it easier to expel and breathe more easily.

Leaves: Rosemary leaves contain antioxidants and volatile oils that help soothe a sore throat, reduce coughing, and relieve congestion.

Combine the harvested rosemary leaves with one cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant teapot.
Allow the tea mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes before straining off any loose herb particles.
Add honey and lemon juice to taste, if desired, and mix well with a spoon.
Drink the warm rosmarinus officinalis tea immediately and re-steep the leaves as needed every 2 hours throughout the day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger teas helps with throat congestion because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the irritated tissues in the throat. The warm and steamy liquid helps to loosen and clear out mucus, reducing the thickness and stickiness that can exacerbate coughs and sore throats. Additionally, ginger's natural expectorant properties help to bring up phlegm and mucus from the lungs, providing quick relief from congestion and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, help reduce swelling and pain in the throat, alleviating congestion and discomfort.
Expectorant properties: The mucilages and saponins present in ginger tea help loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm from the throat, making it easier to expel and relieving congestion.

Antioxidant and antimicrobial properties: Ginger's antioxidant and antimicrobial compounds help fight off viral and bacterial infections that can cause throat congestion, while also protecting the mucous membranes from damage.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, ginger roots have been traditionally used to reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort associated with throat congestion.
Rhyzomes: The thick, underground stem of the ginger plant contains natural analgesics and anti-inflammatory agents that help ease pain and soothe irritated throats.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 inches of fresh ginger root and peel it thoroughly.
Cut the peeled ginger into small pieces and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Measure out one teaspoon of ground ginger and combine it with boiling water in a teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the ginger's active compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea into a cup and add honey to taste before serving.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea teas helps with throat congestion because of its natural decongestant properties. Herbal teas like peppermint, eucalyptus, and ginger contain active ingredients that help loosen mucus and reduce inflammation in the throat, making it easier to breathe and speak. The soothing warmth of the tea also provides temporary relief from scratchiness and discomfort, allowing the body's natural healing process to take over. Additionally, some herbal teas have antimicrobial properties that can help combat infections causing congestion.
remedy_properties: Echinacea: Echinacea's anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties help reduce swelling in the throat and combat infections that cause congestion.
Thyme: Thyme's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the throat, making it easier to expel and reducing congestion.
Slippery Elm: Slippery Elm's mucilaginous properties soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the throat, reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in expectorant properties, roots help loosen and remove mucus from the respiratory tract.
Buds: Containing antibacterial compounds, buds have anti-inflammatory effects that reduce swelling in the throat and combat infection.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry 2 tablespoons of camellia sinensis leaves or use store-bought loose-leaf tea according to manufacturer's instructions.
Boil 1 cup of water and pour it over the dried tea leaves in a teapot or infuser for 5 minutes.
Steep the tea mixture for an additional 10 minutes, allowing the herbal properties to release and mingle with the hot water.
Strain the tea into a large mug using a spoon or tea strainer, discarding any solids and leaving behind the flavorful liquid.
Allow the tea to steep for at least 10-15 minutes before drinking, enabling the soothing properties to take effect on the throat congestion.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder teas helps with throat congestion because it contains natural expectorants that loosen and clear mucus from the throat, reducing discomfort and coughing. The antiviral properties in elder flowers also help combat underlying infections that can cause congestion, while the soothing warmth of the tea relaxes the throat muscles and eases inflammation. Additionally, the antioxidants present in elder teas have anti-inflammatory effects, which can further reduce swelling and promote healing in the throat.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial compounds: Elder tea contains antimicrobial compounds such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which help to combat bacterial and viral infections that cause throat congestion.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The polyphenols and anthocyanins present in elder tea have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the throat, providing relief from congestion.
Expectorant activity: Elder tea contains expectorants such as mucilage, which helps to loosen and thin mucus, making it easier to cough up and relieve congested throats.
remedy_parts: Roots: Elder roots are used due to their natural expectorant properties, helping to loosen and remove mucus from the throat.
Barks: Elder barks have been traditionally used to soothe sore throats and reduce inflammation, making them a common ingredient in teas for throat congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried sambucus nigra flowers and leaves in sufficient quantities.
Combine one teaspoon of dried sambucus nigra mixture or two teaspoons of fresh mixture.
Add boiling water to the mixture allowing it to steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea using a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a cup or teapot.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before consuming and re-brewing as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel teas helps with throat congestion because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The tea's active compounds, such as fumaric acid and flavonoids, help to reduce swelling and soothe the mucous membranes in the throat, making it easier to breathe and swallow. Additionally, fennel's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out mucus, allowing for a more efficient removal of trapped airways. This natural remedy provides quick relief from congestion, making it an effective alternative to over-the-counter medications.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Fennel tea's antispasmodic compounds relax the muscles in the throat, reducing coughing and relieving discomfort caused by inflammation.
Expectorant properties: The volatile oils present in fennel tea help loosen and clear mucus from the throat, making it easier to expel and relieving congestion.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and redness in the throat, soothing discomfort and promoting healing of irritated tissues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots are used due to their natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and calm irritated throats.
Seeds: Fennel seeds are used as a natural expectorant, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried fennel seeds and boiling water to create the tea.
Combine the fennel seeds with one cup of boiling water in a teapot or infuser.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes to release the medicinal properties.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup, discarding the solids.
Enjoy the tea hot, sweetened with honey if desired, and let it soothe your throat congestion.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender teas helps with throat congestion because of its natural antihistamine properties, which alleviate inflammation and reduce excess mucus production. The calming effects of lavender also soothe the throat, making it easier to breathe and swallow. Additionally, the tea's antibacterial compounds help combat underlying infections that may be contributing to congestion. By promoting relaxation and reducing swelling, English lavender teas provide effective relief from a scratchy, congested throat.
remedy_properties: Antihistamine and anti-inflammatory properties: These properties help to reduce swelling and itching in the throat, providing relief from congestion and discomfort.
Expectorant properties: English lavender tea contains compounds that help loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm from the respiratory tract, making it easier to cough up and breathe.

Calming and soothing effects: The calming properties of English lavender tea can help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for throat congestion, and provide a sense of relaxation to the respiratory system.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in essential oils, including linalool and linalyl acetate, which help soothe and calm irritated throats.
Flowers: Containing flavonoids and terpenes that have anti-inflammatory properties, reducing swelling and pain associated with throat congestion.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried lavender flowers per eight ounces of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for seven to nine minutes to allow the flavonoids and terpenes release.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solid lavender particles.
Add honey to taste if desired for sweetness and soothe sore throat irritation instantly.
Drink the tea immediately after preparation while still warm to aid in congestion relief.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to throat congestion that are treated with herbal teas are respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and coughs. Herbal teas like eucalyptus, peppermint, and thyme have natural decongestant properties that help relieve chest congestion and ease breathing. Additionally, teas containing slippery elm, marshmallow root, and licorice root soothe sore throats and reduce inflammation, providing relief from these respiratory issues.
intro_desc: Herbal teas for throat congestion are natural remedies that help alleviate symptoms of throat congestion by soothing and calming the throat, reducing inflammation, and promoting relaxation. The best herbal teas for throat congestion include Glycyrrhiza glabra, Echinacea purpurea, Eucalyptus globulus, Thymus vulgaris, Zingiber officinale, Sambucus nigra, Mentha x piperita, Lavandula angustifolia, Rosmarinus officinalis, and Hyptis suaveolens. If left untreated, throat congestion can lead to discomfort, difficulty speaking, and loss of sleep, impacting daily life and productivity, causing frustration and stress.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal teas that help with throat congestion is a blend of peppermint, eucalyptus, and licorice root. Peppermint tea's cooling properties and menthol content help to numb the throat, while eucalyptus tea's decongestant properties loosen mucus and ease breathing. Licorice root tea, with its anti-inflammatory properties, soothes and calms the throat, reducing swelling and pain. Drinking these teas together can provide quick relief from throat congestion and promote overall respiratory health.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Journal of alternative and complementary medicine (New York, N.Y.), a traditional demulcent herbal tea called Throat Coat was found to be effective in alleviating throat congestion symptoms. The study, a multicenter, prospective, randomized, double-blinded, placebo-controlled clinical trial, involved 60 patients with acute pharyngitis who were randomly assigned to receive either Throat Coat or a placebo tea. The results showed that Throat Coat significantly reduced the intensity of throat pain when swallowing, with a statistically significant improvement in pain relief (SPID) and total pain relief (TOTPAR) in the Throat Coat-treated group compared to the placebo group.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas helps with throat congestion because of its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The active compounds in licorice root, such as glycyrrhizin, have been shown to reduce inflammation and swelling in the throat, making it easier to swallow and breathe. Additionally, the expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, providing quick relief from congestion. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to soothe a sore throat and promote respiratory health.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: Helps alleviate throat congestion by reducing inflammation and soothing the mucous membranes in the throat.
Liquiritigenin: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which aid in relieving throat congestion and promoting a healthy respiratory system.

18β-Glycyrrhetinic acid: Demonstrates anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, helping to reduce throat congestion by modulating the immune response and alleviating inflammation in the respiratory tract.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots: The primary part used to make teas due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps soothe and reduce throat congestion.
Leaves: Leaves: These parts are used in teas, often in combination with roots, to enhance their medicinal properties and provide additional anti-inflammatory effects.
Barks: Barks: Barks may also be used to make teas, though less commonly than roots, offering a complementary set of compounds that contribute to their therapeutic benefits.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 teaspoons of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and place them in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Boil 8 ounces of water in a kettle or on the stovetop for 3-5 minutes to reach optimal temperature.
Steep the dried roots in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes or until the desired flavor is achieved.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup or teapot to remove the roots.
Serve the tea hot or chilled and enjoy within 30 minutes after preparation to maximize benefits.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas helps with throat congestion because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the throat. The tea also acts as an expectorant, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea contains compounds that stimulate the immune system, which can help to fight off underlying infections that may be contributing to the congestion. This natural remedy can provide relief from throat congestion and promote overall respiratory health.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help reduce inflammation in the throat, thereby alleviating congestion and discomfort.
Phenylethylamine (PEA) alkaloids: PEA alkaloids exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in soothing and calming the throat, making it easier to breathe.
Iridoids (including Cichoric acid): These compounds have been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling in the throat and alleviate congestion.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Echinacea flowers are commonly used due to their high content of alkylamides, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce throat congestion.
Roots: Echinacea roots are often used for their immune-boosting properties, which help the body fight off infections that cause throat congestion.
Leaves: Echinacea leaves are commonly used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and calm an irritated throat.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the fresh echinacea purpurea flowers and roots in the morning when they are dry.
Dry the harvested flowers and roots in a warm place with good air circulation for 1 week.
Grind the dried flowers and roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Steep 1 teaspoon of the echinacea powder in boiling water for 5-7 minutes to make the tea.
Strain the tea and serve it hot or cold after 10-15 minutes of preparation time.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus teas helps with throat congestion because of its decongestant properties. The tea contains compounds like eucalyptol, which work to break down mucus and ease respiratory issues. When inhaled, eucalyptol helps to thin out mucus, making it easier to expel, providing relief from stuffy noses and sore throats. Additionally, the tea's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm the throat, reducing discomfort and allowing for easier breathing. This makes Eucalyptus globulus tea a popular remedy for colds and flu.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): It acts as a bronchodilator, helping to relieve congestion by expanding the airways and making it easier to breathe.
Caffic acid: It has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling in the throat and alleviate discomfort associated with congestion.
β-Caryophyllene: It is a natural expectorant, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to cough up and relieve congestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the primary part used to make Eucalyptus tea due to their high concentration of eucalyptol, an expectorant compound that helps relieve congestion.
Barks: Eucalyptus barks are also used, as they contain a higher concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that aid in soothing throat irritation.
Stems: Eucalyptus stems, which contain a smaller amount of eucalyptol compared to leaves, are sometimes used as a substitute or in combination with leaves to enhance the tea's expectorant properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves and stems with sharp scissors or pruning shears for optimal flavor.
Rinse the harvested eucalyptus leaves gently with cool water to remove dirt and debris quickly.
Combine 1-2 teaspoons of fresh eucalyptus leaves with 8 ounces of boiling water in a teapot or infuser.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes allowing the eucalyptus flavors to infuse into the water slowly.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the eucalyptus leaves completely.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris teas helps with throat congestion because it contains thymol, a natural decongestant and antimicrobial agent. The active compounds in thyme, such as carvacrol and linalool, work to break down mucus and reduce inflammation in the throat. As a result, thymus vulgaris teas helps to relieve coughs and sore throats by thinning mucus and promoting easier breathing. This herbal remedy has been used for centuries to alleviate respiratory issues, making it a popular choice for those looking for a natural solution.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Acts as an expectorant, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the throat, making it easier to breathe.
Carvacrol: Possesses antimicrobial properties, reducing the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that can contribute to throat infections and congestion.
Rosmarinic acid: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, reducing swelling and redness in the throat and helping to alleviate discomfort associated with congestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thyme leaves are commonly used to make teas due to their high thymol content, which is known for its antimicrobial properties that help alleviate throat congestion.
Flowers: Thyme flowers are used to make teas because they contain oils that help to soothe and calm the throat, reducing inflammation and congestion.
Stems: Thyme stems are used to make teas as they contain essential oils that have expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the throat and lungs.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried thymus vulgaris leaves and 1 cup of boiling water for steeping time.
Steep the dried herb in boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes to release the flavors.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove solids.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to sweeten the tea if desired for taste purposes only.
Store the cooled tea in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours in an airtight container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale teas helps with throat congestion because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The active compound, gingerol, in Zingiber officinale has been shown to reduce swelling and ease pain in the throat. Additionally, the tea's warm and soothing nature helps to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe. The expectorant properties of Zingiber officinale also help to thin and expel mucus, providing quick relief from throat congestion. This makes Zingiber officinale tea a popular remedy for colds and respiratory issues.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Gingerols have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat, making it easier to breathe and alleviating discomfort.
Shogaols: Shogaols have decongestant properties that help break down mucus and phlegm in the throat, making it easier to expel and relieving congestion.
Zingiberene: Zingiberene has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help reduce inflammation and infection in the throat, promoting healing and relieving symptoms of congestion.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The most commonly used part of ginger, rhyzomes are dried and then used to make tea due to their high concentration of active compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which help alleviate throat congestion.
Stems: Some traditional remedies use ginger stems to make tea, but they are less commonly used than rhyzomes.
Buds: Ginger buds can also be used to make tea, but they are not as widely used as rhyzomes or stems.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes with a length of about 1 inch and a thickness of 0.5 inch.
Peel the harvested rhizomes using a vegetable peeler to remove the outer skin completely.
Slice the peeled rhizomes into thin pieces about 0.25 inch thick using a sharp knife.
Steep 2-3 slices of the sliced rhizome in 8 oz of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release the active compounds.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup to remove the solids completely.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra teas helps with throat congestion because of its natural antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea contains high levels of compounds like sambunigrin and sambucol, which have been shown to inhibit the replication of viruses that cause respiratory infections. By reducing inflammation and soothing the mucous membranes, Sambucus nigra teas can help alleviate symptoms of a sore throat, such as pain, swelling, and difficulty swallowing. This makes it a popular natural remedy for colds and flu.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help reduce inflammation and soothe the throat, making it easier to swallow and relieving congestion.
Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids possess antiviral and antimicrobial properties, which help combat viral and bacterial infections causing throat congestion.
Tannins: Tannins have astringent properties that help reduce swelling and dry out excess mucus in the throat, providing quick relief from congestion.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Sambucus nigra flowers are the most commonly used part to make teas for throat congestion due to their high concentration of antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Sambucus nigra leaves are used to make teas for throat congestion as they contain salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which helps reduce inflammation and relieve pain.

Fruits: Sambucus nigra fruits, also known as elderberries, are used to make teas for throat congestion as they have antiviral properties that help combat infections and reduce inflammation in the throat.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 cups of fresh sambucus nigra flowers and leaves when they are in full bloom.
Dry the harvested sambucus nigra flowers and leaves in a warm dark place for 1-2 weeks.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried sambucus nigra flowers and leaves in boiling water for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove the solids.
Enjoy the tea immediately while it is still warm and fragrant for optimal benefits.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita teas helps with throat congestion because of its natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. The menthol content in peppermint oil helps to relax the muscles in the throat, reducing swelling and making it easier to swallow. Additionally, the tea's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, promoting a faster recovery from colds and flu. This combination of properties makes Mentha x piperita teas a popular remedy for soothing sore throats and relieving congestion.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol helps with throat congestion by reducing inflammation and acting as a natural decongestant to ease breathing.
Menthone: Menthone helps with throat congestion by exhibiting expectorant properties, making it easier to cough up mucus and phlegm from the respiratory tract.
Eugenol: Eugenol helps with throat congestion by displaying analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with throat irritation and congestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in menthol, which helps to thin mucus and provide relief from congestion.
Buds: Containing menthol and other oils, they help to ease coughs and reduce inflammation in the throat.
Stems: Similar to leaves, stems are also a source of menthol, which helps to clear congestion and soothe the throat.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of fresh mentha x piperita leaves and flowers from the garden for tea making.
Chop the harvested leaves and flowers into small pieces using kitchen scissors or a sharp knife.
Steep 1 teaspoon of chopped mentha x piperita in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove solids.
Serve the tea immediately and enjoy it hot or iced in a few minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas helps with throat congestion because of its natural anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties. The tea's active compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, work to reduce swelling and ease congestion in the throat. Additionally, the tea's soothing and calming effects can help to relax the airways and promote easier breathing. This can provide relief from symptoms such as coughing, soreness, and difficulty swallowing, making it a popular natural remedy for colds and respiratory issues. Its unique properties make it an effective solution for throat congestion.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Acts as a natural expectorant, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the throat, thereby reducing congestion.
Linalyl acetate: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, reducing swelling and irritation in the throat, which can contribute to congestion.
Lavandulol: Has antimicrobial effects, fighting off bacteria and other pathogens that can cause infections and exacerbate throat congestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to make teas due to their soothing and calming properties that help to reduce inflammation and ease throat discomfort.
Flowers: Used to make teas due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that help to combat infections and reduce swelling in the throat.
Stems: Used to make teas due to their antioxidant properties that help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the throat.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25 fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers from your garden or a local supplier at dawn.
Rinse the lavandula angustifolia flowers gently with filtered water for 2 minutes to remove impurities.
Steep 1 tablespoon of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers or 2 tablespoons of fresh flowers in 8 ounces boiling water.
Allow the lavandula angustifolia tea to steep for 5-7 minutes to release its essential oils and flavors.
Strain the lavandula angustifolia tea and discard the solids then serve and enjoy immediately.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis teas helps with throat congestion because of its decongestant and expectorant properties. The tea contains compounds such as camphor and borneol, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of Rosmarinus officinalis help to reduce swelling in the throat, providing relief from discomfort and pain. This natural remedy can provide quick relief for those suffering from colds, flu, and allergies. It's a soothing and effective way to calm a congested throat.
remedy_constituents: Carnosic acid: This diterpene compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and ease congestion in the throat.
Rosmarinic acid: As a phenolic compound, it exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which help combat infections and reduce inflammation in the throat.
Cineole (Eucalyptol): This terpene has expectorant properties, which help loosen and clear mucus from the throat, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used in teas for throat congestion due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the throat.
Flowers: Used in teas for throat congestion due to their decongestant and expectorant properties that help relieve congestion and promote mucus discharge.
Stems: Used in teas for throat congestion due to their ability to reduce inflammation and provide a soothing effect on the throat.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves from your garden or a nearby field.
Dry the harvested leaves for 2-3 hours in a low-temperature oven or a dehydrator at 95 degrees fahrenheit.
Grind 1 teaspoon of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar.
Steep 1 teaspoon of ground rosmarinus officinalis in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and let it cool for 10-15 minutes before consuming in moderation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hyptis suaveolens teas helps with throat congestion because of its natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea, also known as "sweet hyptis" or "vaca muerta," contains essential oils that help loosen mucus and reduce swelling in the throat. Its expectorant properties aid in coughing up excess mucus, providing relief from congestion and making breathing easier. Additionally, the tea's soothing effects calm the throat, allowing for a smoother flow of air and a reduction in discomfort associated with throat congestion.
remedy_constituents: Monoterpenes: Help reduce inflammation and relieve congestion in the throat by acting as a natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory agent.
Flavonoids: Possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and soothe the irritated tissues in the throat, providing relief from congestion.
Eugenol: Act as a natural expectorant, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the throat, making it easier to breathe and relieving congestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties to help relieve congestion and reduce throat irritation.
Flowers: Used for their decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties to help reduce swelling and promote a soothing relief in the throat.
Roots: Used for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties to help reduce infection and promote healing in the throat.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh hyptis suaveolens leaves and flowers in the early morning when they are at their highest oil content.
Dry the harvested hyptis suaveolens leaves and flowers in a warm place for 24 to 48 hours.
Steep 2g of dried hyptis suaveolens leaves and flowers in 200ml of boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the hyptis suaveolens tea through a fine mesh to remove the solids from the liquid.
Store the hyptis suaveolens tea in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before serving.
